content.full_text:
December 17, 1993

Cynthia L. Humbert
Assistant City Attorney
City of Stockton
425 North El Dorado Street
Stockton, CA 95202-1997

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-455

Dear Ms. Humbert:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
December 6, 1993 wherein we discussed your request for advice on
behalf of Stockton City Councilmember Nick Rust regarding his
duties under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹

According to your facts, in February or March of 1994, the
city council will hear a proposal for a General Plan Amendment and
Rezoning involving Food for Less, a grocery establishment which is
owned by Dodie, Inc. ("Dodie"). Mr. Gary Podesto is President of
Dodie and his family owns 100 percent of the corporation.
Mr. Podesto retained Councilmember Rust to provide personnel
training for management staff of Food for Less in early 1993.
Mr. Rust completed the job and was paid $1300 for these services
by check dated February 19, 1993 from Dodie. Mr. Rust received
the check on February 22 and cashed the check on
February 24, 1993.

I advised you that pursuant to Section 87103(c), Dodie is
considered to be a source of income to Mr. Rust for a period of
twelve months after the date he was paid by Dodie. Therefore, if
the city council votes on the General Plan Amendment and Rezoning
in March of 1994, Dodie is no longer considered a source of income
under the Act, provided that Mr. Rust has been paid in full and
has provided no further services.

I further advised you that a council decision to delay the
vote on this issue to March 1994 is not considered ministerial and

_______________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-93-455
Page 2


Mr. Rust should not participate in the decision regarding the date
of the council meeting.

I trust this answers your question.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        Jill [illegible]
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Stockton City Councilmember Nick Rust must recuse himself from voting on a General Plan Amendment and Rezoning proposal for Food for Less in March 1994, given that he received $1,300 in payment from the company's owner in February 1993 for personnel training services.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under Section 87103(c), Dodie (Food for Less's parent company) is considered a source of income for twelve months after the date of payment (February 1993), meaning by March 1994 it would no longer be a source of income requiring recusal, provided no further services were rendered. However, any decision to delay the vote to March 1994 is non-ministerial and Councilmember Rust should not participate in determining the council meeting date.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Telephone advice letter confirming prior oral discussion. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections found; advice provided in body of letter. Extracted question and conclusion from substantive content discussing Section 87103(c) application and timing analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Stockton City Councilmember Nick Rust must recuse himself from voting on a General Plan Amendment and Rezoning proposal for Food for Less in March 1994, given that he received $1,300 in payment from the company's owner in February 1993 for personnel training services.

CONCLUSION: Under Section 87103(c), Dodie (Food for Less's parent company) is considered a source of income for twelve months after the date of payment (February 1993), meaning by March 1994 it would no longer be a source of income requiring recusal, provided no further services were rendered. However, any decision to delay the vote to March 1994 is non-ministerial and Councilmember Rust should not participate in determining the council meeting date.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel advises that a Stockton City Councilmember who received payment in February 1993 from a company for services may vote on that company's rezoning proposal in March 1994, as the twelve-month income source period will have expired, but must recuse himself from decisions about scheduling the vote.
